new_metric.name: New Metric
new_metric.description: New metric used to test parameters: uses first numeric column in Test Dataset

params.label: Threshold
params.help: If computed value > threshold, return threshold; otherwise return computed value.

conditional_statistical_parity.name: Conditional Statistical Parity
conditional_statistical_parity.description: Measures disparity in selection rates across sensitive groups conditioned on another variable.

params.label: Sensitive features
params.help: Select one or more sensitive feature columns (e.g., sex, age).

params.label: Conditional variable
params.help: Column name used to condition the fairness comparison.

conditional_use_accuracy_equality.name: Conditional Use Accuracy Equality
conditional_use_accuracy_equality.description: Accuracy parity across sensitive groups, computed only on samples with positive predictions (ŷ=1).
conditional_use_accuracy_equality.interpretation: Values near 0 reflects equal reliability of model decisions.

params.label: Sensitive features
params.help: Select one or more sensitive feature columns (e.g., sex, age).

demographic_parity.name: Demographic Parity
demographic_parity.description: Measures demographic parity by computing selection rate differences across sensitive groups.
demographic_parity.interpretation: Values close to 1 indicate better demographic parity (less disparity) between groups.

params.label: Sensitive features
params.help: Select one or more sensitive feature columns (e.g., sex, age).

disparate_impact.name: Disparate Impact
disparate_impact.description: Disparate Impact based on selection rates across sensitive groups (min SR / max SR).
disparate_impact.interpretation: Values near 1 indicates fairness, while values below 0.8 often signal potential discrimination.

params.label: Sensitive features
params.help: Select one or more sensitive feature columns (e.g., sex, age).

equal_opportunity.name: Equal Opportunity
equal_opportunity.description: Measures Equal Opportunity by comparing True Positive Rates across sensitive groups.
equal_opportunity.interpretation: Values close to 0 means equal opportunity is preserved.

params.label: Sensitive features
params.help: Select one or more sensitive feature columns (e.g., sex, age).

equalized_odds_difference.name: Equalized Odds Difference
equalized_odds_difference.description: Measures Equalized Odds difference across sensitive groups (lower is better).
equalized_odds_difference.interpretation: Values near 0 indicate prediction errors are evenly distributed.

params.label: Sensitive features
params.help: Select one or more sensitive feature columns (e.g., sex, age).

overall_accuracy_equality.name: Overall Accuracy Equality
overall_accuracy_equality.description: Compares accuracy across sensitive groups and reports difference/ratio (higher equality_score is better).
overall_accuracy_equality.interpretation: Values close to 0 indicate uniform predictive performance.

params.label: Sensitive features
params.help: Select one or more sensitive feature columns (e.g., sex, age).

predictive_parity.name: Predictive Parity
predictive_parity.description: Compares precision across sensitive groups (higher equality means smaller precision gap).
predictive_parity.interpretation: A 0 difference means the model is equally reliable when predicting positives.

params.label: Sensitive features
params.help: Select one or more sensitive feature columns (e.g., sex, age).

anonymity_set_size.name: Anonymity Set Size
anonymity_set_size.description: Computes anonymity set sizes for combinations of sensitive features (quasi-identifiers).

params.label: Sensitive features
params.help: Select one or more sensitive feature columns (e.g., sex, age).

k_anonymity.name: K-Anonymity
k_anonymity.description: Evaluates K-Anonymity over quasi-identifiers (each equivalence class must have at least k records).

params.label: k value
params.help: Minimum group size for k-anonymity compliance.

params.label: Quasi-identifiers
params.help: Columns used to form equivalence classes. If empty, falls back to the selected sensitive_features.

l_diversity.name: L-Diversity
l_diversity.description: Computes L-Diversity by grouping on quasi-identifiers and counting distinct values of a sensitive attribute.

params.label: Quasi-identifiers
params.help: Columns used to form groups. If empty, falls back to selected sensitive_features.

params.label: Sensitive attribute
params.help: Column name whose diversity within each group is measured.

params.label: l value
params.help: Minimum number of distinct sensitive values required in each equivalence class.

mutual_information.name: Mutual Information
mutual_information.description: Computes Mutual Information between each feature and a selected sensitive attribute.

params.label: Sensitive Attribute
params.help: Column used as target variable for Mutual Information computation.

params.label: Columns to Exclude
params.help: Columns excluded from MI computation (besides the sensitive attribute).

t_closeness.name: T-Closeness
t_closeness.description: Computes Wasserstein distance between global and group distributions of a sensitive attribute, grouped by quasi-identifiers.

params.label: Quasi-identifiers
params.help: Columns used to form groups. If empty, falls back to selected sensitive_features.

params.label: Sensitive attribute
params.help: Column whose distribution is compared globally vs within each group.
